name: Kandev

runner.notes: Install from upstream docs, prepare a run worktree, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification.

inclusion: Direct open-source ADE competitor with kanban, agents, worktrees, and self-hosting emphasis.
public_stack_signals: GitHub repository is AGPL-3.0 and describes no telemetry, self-hosting, multi-provider agents, code review, PRs, and worktrees.
Repository metadata identifies Go as the primary language and topics include Next.js, self-hosted, kanban, ACP, workflow automation, and worktrees.
Feature docs describe Go backend, Next.js frontend, CLI, and Tauri desktop surfaces.
setup_notes: Benchmark with a local/self-hosted install and document executor choice.

sources.notes: Repository description, license, topics, installation docs, and implementation signals.
Top-level product positioning and no-telemetry/self-hosting claims.
Feature list covering kanban, ACP agents, executors, worktrees, integrations, and app surfaces.

research.positioning: Open-source AI kanban and development environment for orchestrating multiple agents, reviewing changes, and opening PRs.

data: local database under user home according to setup docs
unknowns: Exact production database/storage choices should be verified from source or install docs before comparing infrastructure cost.
Maturity of desktop packaging needs hands-on install.

infrastructure: Tasks can execute via local, Docker, SSH, and Sprites-style executors according to feature docs.
Worktree-centric task isolation across repositories and branches.
No telemetry claim in upstream README.

remote_mobile: self-hosted server reachable through the user's network/VPN setup
no dedicated mobile companion mapped in official docs reviewed

parity_risks: Server/workflow product may outperform on task queue/review operations not covered by v0.1 single-worktree tasks.
Executor choice can affect speed, isolation, and reproducibility; record it in run metadata.
Some features may require integrations or self-hosted setup beyond local smoke.

hypotheses: Kanban-native workflow gives a clearer operations model for many concurrent tasks.
Self-hosted/no-telemetry posture appeals to teams that cannot use SaaS ADE control planes.
ACP plus executor breadth may make it easier to plug in heterogeneous agent and runtime setups.
evidence: Upstream repository description states orchestration, review, PRs, multi-provider, self-hostable, and no telemetry.
Feature docs list workflow boards, ACP agents, worktrees, executors, integrations, and MCP.
unknowns: How much review/evidence Kandev captures compared with Tachyon should be measured, not inferred.
